file_name: PSGT Agreement NDA New Supplier Non Disclosure Template DC.txt

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Supplier is requir ed to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to the requirements of applicab le law, rules, regulations, court or other governmental authority, the Supplier shall provide pr ompt advance notice (in wr iting, if practical) to CGSH in order to enable CGSH to contes t such requirement of disclosure or seek an appropriate protective order, and shall disclose su ch Confidential Information only to the extent the Supplier reasonably determines it is lega lly compelled to disclose such Confidential Information and with respect to wh ich it agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treat ment will be accorded therefor.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Supplier is requir ed to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to the requirements of applicab le law, rules, regulations, court or other governmental authority, the Supplier shall provide pr ompt advance notice (in wr iting, if practical) to CGSH. However, the supplier has no obligation to contest or prevent the disclosure, unless explicitly stated in a seperate agreement. The original text requires a prompt notice, while in the other hand it removes the obligation to contest the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the Supplier's obligation to contest the disclosure of Confidential Information when legally compelled. The original text requires the Supplier to provide notice to CGSH 'in order to enable CGSH to contest' and implies an obligation to cooperate. The change explicitly states the Supplier has no obligation to contest. This undermines CGSH's ability to protect its Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Supplier is requir ed to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to the requirements of applicab le law, rules, regulations, court or other governmental authority, the Supplier shall provide pr ompt advance notice (in wr iting, if practical) to CGSH. However, the supplier has no obligation to contest or prevent the disclosure, unless explicitly stated in a seperate agreement, and shall disclose su ch Confidential Information only to the extent the Supplier reasonably determines it is lega lly compelled to disclose such Confidential Information and with respect to wh ich it agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treat ment will be accorded therefor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text requires the Supplier to provide notice to CGSH 'in order to enable CGSH to contest' implying an obligation to cooperate. The change explicitly states the Supplier has no obligation to contest, creating an in-text contradiction as it undermines CGSH's ability to protect its Confidential Information. This directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section as the changed section removes the requirement for the supplier to contest or prevent the disclosure which is implied in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information. The destruction certification only occurs when requested by the Disclosing Party. Originally the certification was mandatory.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by making the delivery of destruction certification optional. Previously the certification was mandatory. Now, the destruction certification occurs 'only when requested by the Disclosing Party'. This change creates uncertainty about whether the Confidential Information was destroyed or not. The agreement seeks the mandatory destruction/return with confirmation, which is not achieved with this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information. The destruction certification only occurs when requested by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination, the Supplier 'shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction)'. The changed text makes the certification optional ('The destruction certification only occurs when requested by the Disclosing Party.'). This directly contradicts the original mandatory requirement for certification, introducing uncertainty and potential unenforceability. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthori zed use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and for which such Disclosing Party may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, wi thout the posting of any bond or security or the giving of any undertaking as to damages or otherwi se, specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent juri sdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized us e or disclosure, whether threatened or actual. The rights of CGSH set forth in this paragraph shall be in addition to, and without prejudice to, any other rights that CGSH may have her eunder, whether at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthori zed use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and for which such Disclosing Party may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, wi th the posting of a reasonable bond or security , specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent juri sdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized us e or disclosure, whether threatened or actual. The rights of CGSH set forth in this paragraph shall be in addition to, and without prejudice to, any other rights that CGSH may have her eunder, whether at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by now requiring the posting of a bond or security. Originally, CGSH could seek equitable relief 'without the posting of any bond or security'. The change forces CGSH to provide a 'reasonable bond or security' which is subject to interpretation and can create disagreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthori zed use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and for which such Disclosing Party may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, wi th the posting of a reasonable bond or security , specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent juri sdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized us e or disclosure, whether threatened or actual. The rights of CGSH set forth in this paragraph shall be in addition to, and without prejudice to, any other rights that CGSH may have her eunder, whether at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that CGSH can seek equitable relief 'without the posting of any bond or security'. The changed text introduces the requirement of 'with the posting of a reasonable bond or security'. This directly contradicts the original statement, as now CGSH is obligated to post a bond, which it was previously exempt from, causing an inconsistency as defined above.
